mechanism_narrative: Sustained high retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand and corporate revenue growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negatively correlated with RSAFS levels over a 3-year rolling window (rank IC < 0.02) or the top-quartile RSAFS months show lower median returns than bottom-quartile months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises sharply above 6.5%, the labor market deterioration signals falling disposable income and consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as markets reprice recession risk.
failure_condition: In >50% of triggered instances the S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns, or the average return after trigger is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) declining together indicate simultaneous contraction in output and employment, signaling a weakening economy that reduces corporate profits and forward equity returns.
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline month-over-month, the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns more than 40% of the time or the mean return is above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below the year-over-year inflation rate from CPI (CPIAUCSL) creates negative real interest rates, which compress discount rates and boost equity valuations over a multi-month horizon as investors search for yield.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day returns in negative real rate periods are not significantly higher than in positive real rate periods (two-sample t-test p > 0.05) over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at elevated levels signal strong construction activity and household investment, which leads economic expansions and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon as cyclical sectors outperform.
failure_condition: Top-quartile HOUST months yield lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than bottom-quartile months; rank IC < 0.03 over 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) drops below the 100 index level, the manufacturing sector is contracting, which signals declining aggregate supply and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Instances where INDPRO < 100 are followed by positive 5-day returns in >50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicate a virtuous cycle of rising consumer spending and labor income, which drives corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: In months when both RSAFS and PAYEMS rise month-over-month, the S&P 500 delivers negative 20-day returns more than 40% of the time or the mean return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment (UNRATE) is high and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is near zero, the market expects aggressive monetary accommodation to support labor markets, which lifts equity valuations over a quarterly horizon as the policy put is priced in.
failure_condition: 60-day returns in the triggered regime are not significantly positive (one-sample t-test p > 0.05) or are lower than returns in non-triggered periods over any 3-year window.
